Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The DECLARE model provided init R must start with Receive existence C must contain Close responded existence E to A if Evaluate exists Assign must exist precedence C to R if Close exists Receive must precede it noncoexistence E C Evaluate and Close cannot coexist

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The existence constraint requires C Close to exist

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The intended process flow requires E Evaluate and P Approve before N Notify and C Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The noncoexistence constraint says E and C cannot coexist This means if a claim is evaluated E it cannot be closed C But since C is required by existence E can never happen in a compliant trace Or if E happens C cannot happen violating existence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait if E and C cannot coexist and C is mandatory then E is effectively forbidden

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This contradicts the intended process flow where evaluation is a necessary step before approval and closing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The responded existence E to A says if Evaluate happens Assign must happen But if E is practically forbidden by the noncoexistence with C which is mandatory then E is dead making this rule moot

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The precedence C to R is fine C must be preceded by R but it misses the other intermediate steps A E P N

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Misinterpretation The noncoexistence between E and C might have been intended to mean E and C cannot happen at the exact same time or E cannot happen after C but was modeled as strict noncoexistence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Policy changes Maybe a new policy allows auto closing claims without evaluation but the old system still has remnants of evaluation rules

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Incomplete data The system generating the DECLARE model might have seen traces where claims were closed without evaluation leading the discovery algorithm to infer noncoexistence between E and C

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Find claims where evaluation and closing coexist violating the rule

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Find claims where evaluation happened but no assigner was recorded or no A activity
